seoDescription: St. Francis Barber Shop is an old-fashioned, walk-in barber shop in Rincon Valley, Santa Rosa, established in 1963 — men's cuts from flat tops to zero fades, plus shaves and beard trims by licensed barbers.
highlights: Old-fashioned cuts at a reasonable price
aboutBody: St. Francis Barber Shop has been your friendly neighborhood barber since 1963 — a classic, old-fashioned shop in the St. Francis/Safeway center in Rincon Valley, at the corner of Highway 12 and Calistoga Road.
It's a walk-in shop with a handful of chairs, where licensed barbers handle everything from flat tops to zero fades, plus straight shaves and beard trims, all at a reasonable price.

services.title: Men's Haircuts
services.description: All forms of men's cuts, from classic flat tops to modern zero fades, by licensed barbers.

services.title: Fades & Tapers
services.description: Clean, sharp fades and tapers tailored to how you like to wear it.

services.title: Shaves
services.description: Traditional barbershop shaves done the old-fashioned way.

services.title: Beard Trims
services.description: Beard shaping and trims to keep your look tidy between cuts.

testimonials.quote: The barbers at the shop are very good at what they do. The price is reasonable.

notes: VERIFIED by web search (chamberofcommerce, businessyab, yellowpages, their live Weebly site). REAL FACTS: 120 Calistoga Rd, Santa Rosa, CA 95409 (St. Francis/Safeway center, Rincon Valley, corner of Hwy 12 & Calistoga Rd, breezeway near Subway/UPS). Phone (707) 539-3475 (an alt (707) 470-6781 also appears). Est. 1963. Walk-in only. Tagline 'Your friendly neighborhood barber'. Services: men's cuts (flat tops to zero fades), shaves, beard trims, licensed barbers; cash-only noted on some listings. Hours Tue–Sat 9–5:30, closed Sun/Mon. Instagram @st.francisbarber. AMBIGUITY/needs-review: conflicting ownership signals — older reviews name owner 'Jayne'; a 2023 search result claims the location was sold to 'Jason' and rebranded 'Mr. Barber Shop'. The St. Francis site/identity is still live, so built under the St. Francis name, but DO NOT name an owner and verify current ownership/name before sending. No usable photos on the JS-rendered site and none verifiable as theirs — built photo-free editorial (barber motif) per the no-wrong-hero rule. Phone, cash-only policy, and exact rating/review count unconfirmed — left conservative.
